paper_ready_status: paper-ready within studied four-phase digital IQCOT implementation
safe_claim: Ton_diff is the primary DC current-sharing actuator in the studied four-phase digital IQCOT model, but it must be constrained for phase spacing, ripple, voltage error, and sensing confidence.
unsafe_claim: Ton_diff solves all transient problems; Ton_diff can be used without phase/ripple/voltage cost; hardware has validated the Ton actuator.
required_next_validation: R052 should consolidate Ton resolution and timing-cost budget; broader controller-performance claims need one guarded selector validation, not a full matrix.
result_if_supported: Ton_diff remains the main current-sharing channel with guard-limited use
result_if_not_supported: Ton_diff wording must be downgraded to preliminary or limited to the already tested mismatch chunk

paper_ready_status: paper-ready as a phase/event actuator and non-strong DC sharing channel
current_evidence_strength: strong analytic + per-phase IEK + load sweep + E030 Lambda-only ablation
safe_claim: Lambda_diff mainly affects phase spacing, event rhythm, and ripple-cancellation neighborhood behavior; it is not a strong DC current-sharing actuator.
unsafe_claim: Lambda_diff is the main current-sharing actuator; Lambda_diff has zero effect on current; active Lambda closed-loop control is validated.
required_next_validation: If active Lambda control is claimed, run a minimal event-native Lambda micro-audit with no REQ pulse loss.
result_if_supported: Lambda_diff can be used as a guarded phase/event boundary variable
result_if_not_supported: Active Lambda claims remain disabled and Lambda wording stays as side-band/logging only

paper_ready_status: paper-ready as disturbance/budget term within model evidence
current_evidence_strength: strong analytic matrix + digital jitter gain summary
safe_claim: delay_diff is best treated as phase jitter, detection timing disturbance, and digital implementation budget rather than a primary supervisory action.
unsafe_claim: delay_diff should be actively controlled by AI as a main current-sharing action; delay tuning replaces Ton/Lambda action design.
required_next_validation: R052_DIGITAL_JITTER_BUDGET_CONSOLIDATION should turn the delay/clock/Ton/area rows into a final budget table.
result_if_supported: Delay becomes part of the paper-ready digital implementation budget
result_if_not_supported: Delay claims must remain a qualitative disturbance statement until R052

current_evidence_strength: medium-strong boundary evidence; controller performance still MODEL_REVISED in E030/R1 and guarded in R3
safe_claim: A projected a_S action can use Ton_diff as the primary current-sharing channel while keeping Lambda as a phase/event boundary or side-band variable; this is a guarded action-space design, not a globally validated controller.
unsafe_claim: C4 globally outperforms Ton_diff-only; active Lambda control is validated; the projected controller is robust across all mismatch families.
required_next_validation: Freeze the guarded a_S selector and validate one smallest additional mismatch or calibration case only if controller-performance claims are needed.
result_if_supported: Combined action can be described as a defensible design interface
result_if_not_supported: Combined controller claim must stay exploratory/conditional while actuator separation remains valid

paper_ready_status: clarified but not validated as AI closed loop
required_next_validation: AI or table supervision should propose low-dimensional bounded parameters such as K_T, T_trim_max, balance_recovery_rate, and optionally K_Lambda/Lambda_trim_max under projection; it must not directly command gates.
result_if_supported: AI directly controls per-cycle Ton_i or gate pulses; AI replaces the IQCOT inner loop; delay_diff is the main AI action.
result_if_not_supported: Before any neural AI claim, validate a rule/table supervisor using the frozen guarded action set.
column9: AI framing stays safe as a parameter-proposal and projection interface
column10: AI performance claims remain future work

paper_ready_status: paper-ready direction but pending R052 consolidation
current_evidence_strength: medium-strong model and Monte Carlo evidence
safe_claim: Quantization, detection clock, Ton resolution, and comparator/supervisor delay map to event jitter, phase-spacing jitter, and current-sharing quantization within PIS-IEK evidence.
unsafe_claim: The digital budget is hardware validated; one budget table proves all FPGA/ASIC implementations.
required_next_validation: Run R052_DIGITAL_JITTER_BUDGET_CONSOLIDATION; no new Simulink full matrix is needed.
result_if_supported: Digital budget becomes the next paper-ready support pillar after actuator separation
result_if_not_supported: Digital budget remains a model-level estimate and should not be overclaimed
